translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God having spoken in many parts and in many ways formerly to the fathers in the prophets,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: at the end of these days has spoken to us in [the person of the] Son, whom he has established heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who being [the] effulgence of his glory and [the] expression of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, having made [by himself] the purification of sins, set himself down on the right hand of the greatness on high,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: taking a place by so much better than the angels, as he inherits a name more excellent than they.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For to which of the angels said he ever, Thou art my Son: this day have I begotten thee? and again, I will be to him for father, and he shall be to me for son?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and again, when he brings in the firstborn into the habitable world, he says, And let allGod's angels worship him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And as to the angels he says, Who makes his angels spirits and his ministers a flame of fire;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but as to the Son, Thy throne, OGod, [is] to the age of the age, and a sceptre of uprightness [is] the sceptre of thy kingdom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast loved righteousness and hast hated lawlessness; thereforeGod, thyGod, has anointed thee with oil of gladness above thy companions.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And, Thou in the beginning, Lord, hast founded the earth, and works of thy hands are the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They shall perish, but thou continuest still; and they all shall grow old as a garment,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and as a covering shalt thou roll them up, and they shall be changed; but thou art the Same, and thy years shall not fail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But as to which of the angels said he ever, Sit at my right hand until I put thine enemies [as] footstool of thy feet?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent out for service on account of those who shall inherit salvation?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this reason we should give heed more abundantly to the things [we have] heard, lest in any way we should slip away.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if the word which was spoken by angels was firm, and every transgression and disobedience received just retribution,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: how shall we escape if we have been negligent of so great salvation, which, having had its commencement in being spoken [of] by the Lord, has been confirmed to us by those who have heard;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God bearing, besides, witness with [them] to [it], both by signs and wonders, and various acts of power, and distributions of [the] Holy Spirit, according to his will?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he has not subjected to angels the habitable world which is to come, of which we speak;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but one has testified somewhere, saying, What is man, that thou rememberest him, or son of man that thou visitest him?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made him some little inferior to the angels; thou hast crowned him with glory and honour, [and hast set him over the works of thy hands;]

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thou hast subjected all things under his feet. For in subjecting all things to him, he has left nothing unsubject to him. But now we see not yet all things subjected to him,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but we see Jesus, who [was] made some little inferior to angels on account of the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; so that by the grace ofGod he should taste death for every thing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it became him, for whom [are] all things, and by whom [are] all things, in bringing many sons to glory, to make perfect the leader of their salvation through sufferings.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For both he that sanctifies and those sanctified [are] all of one; for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brethren,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, I will declare thy name to my brethren; in [the] midst of [the] assembly will I sing thy praises.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And again, I will trust in him. And again, Behold, I and the children whichGod has given me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since therefore the children partake of blood and flesh, he also, in like manner, took part in the same, that through death he might annul him who has the might of death, that is, the devil;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and might set free all those who through fear of death through the whole of their life were subject to bondage.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he does not indeed take hold of angels [by the hand], but he takes hold of the seed of Abraham.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore it behoved him in all things to be made like to [his] brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things relating toGod, to make propitiation for the sins of the people;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for, in that himself has suffered, being tempted, he is able to help those that are being tempted.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of [the] heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Jesus,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who is faithful to him that has constituted him, as Moses also in all his house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he has been counted worthy of greater glory than Moses, by how much he that has built it has more honour than the house.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every house is built by some one; but he who has built all things [is]God.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And Moses indeed [was] faithful in all his house, as a ministering servant, for a testimony of the things to be spoken after;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but Christ, as Son over his house, whose house are we, if indeed we hold fast the boldness and the boast of hope firm to the end.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore, even as says the Holy Spirit, To-day if ye will hear his voice,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: harden not your hearts, as in the provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: where your fathers tempted [me], by proving [me], and saw my works forty years.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore I was wroth with this generation, and said, They always err in heart; and they have not known my ways;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: so I swore in my wrath, If they shall enter into my rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, brethren, lest there be in any one of you a wicked heart of unbelief, in turning away from [the] livingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But encourage yourselves each day, as long as it is called To-day, that none of you be hardened by the deceitfulness of sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we are become companions of the Christ if indeed we hold the beginning of the assurance firm to the end;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: in that it is said, To-day if ye will hear his voice, do not harden your hearts, as in the provocation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for who was it, who, having heard, provoked? but [was it] not all who came out of Egypt by Moses?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And with whom was he wroth forty years? [Was it] not with those who had sinned, whose carcases fell in the wilderness?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to those who had not hearkened to the word?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And we see that they could not enter in on account of unbelief;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left of entering into his rest, any one of you might seem to have failed [of it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For indeed we have had glad tidings presented to us, even as they also; but the word of the report did not profit them, not being mixed with faith in those who heard.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we enter into the rest who have believed; as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, If they shall enter into my rest; although the works had been completed from [the] foundation of [the] world.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he has said somewhere of the seventh [day] thus, AndGod rested on the seventh day from all his works:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and in this again, If they shall enter into my rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Seeing therefore it remains that some enter into it, and those who first received the glad tidings did not enter in on account of not hearkening to the word,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: again he determines a certain day, saying, in David, 'To-day,' after so long a time; (according as it has been said before), To-day, if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if Jesus had brought them into rest, he would not have spoken afterwards about another day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: There remains then a sabbatism to the people ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he that has entered into his rest, he also has rested from his works, asGod did from his own.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us therefore use diligence to enter into that rest, that no one may fall after the same example of not hearkening to the word.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the word ofGod [is] living and operative, and sharper than any two-edged sword, and penetrating to [the] division of soul and spirit, both of joints and marrow, and a discerner of the thoughts and intents of [the] heart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And there is not a creature unapparent before him; but all things [are] naked and laid bare to his eyes, with whom we have to do.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore a great high priest who has passed through the heavens, Jesus the Son ofGod, let us hold fast the confession.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we have not a high priest not able to sympathise with our infirmities, but tempted in all things in like manner, sin apart.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us approach therefore with boldness to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy, and find grace for seasonable help.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every high priest taken from amongst men is established for men in things relating toGod, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: being able to exercise forbearance towards the ignorant and erring, since he himself also is clothed with infirmity;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, on account of this [infirmity], he ought, even as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And no one takes the honour to himself but [as] called byGod, even as Aaron also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thus the Christ also has not glorified himself to be made a high priest; but he who had said to him, Thou art my Son, I have to-day begotten thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even as also in another [place] he says, Thou [art] a priest for ever according to the order of Melchisedec.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who in the days of his flesh, having offered up both supplications and entreaties to him who was able to save him out of death, with strong crying and tears; (and having been heard because of his piety;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: though he were Son, he learned obedience from the things which he suffered;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and having been perfected, became to all them that obey him, author of eternal salvation;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: addressed byGod [as] high priest according to the order of Melchisedec.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Concerning whom we have much to say, and hard to be interpreted in speaking [of it], since ye are become dull in hearing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have again need that [one] should teach you what [are] the elements of the beginning of the oracles ofGod, and are become such as have need of milk, [and] not of solid food.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every one that partakes of milk [is] unskilled in the word of righteousness, for he is a babe;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but solid food belongs to full-grown men, who, on account of habit, have their senses exercised for distinguishing both good and evil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore, leaving the word of the beginning of the Christ, let us go on [to what belongs] to full growth, not laying again a foundation of repentance from dead works and faith inGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of [the] doctrine of washings, and of imposition of hands, and of resurrection of [the] dead, and of eternal judgment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and this will we do ifGod permit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is impossible to renew again to repentance those once enlightened, and who have tasted of the heavenly gift, and have been made partakers of [the] Holy Spirit,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and have tasted the good word ofGod, and [the] works of power of [the] age to come,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and have fallen away, crucifying for themselves [as they do] the Son ofGod, and making a show of [him].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ground which drinks the rain which comes often upon it, and produces useful herbs for those for whose sakes also it is tilled, partakes of blessing fromGod;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but bringing forth thorns and briars, it is found worthless and nigh to a curse, whose end [is] to be burned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we are persuaded concerning you, beloved, better things, and connected with salvation, even if we speak thus.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod [is] not unrighteous to forget your work, and the love which ye have shewn to his name, having ministered to the saints, and [still] ministering.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we desire earnestly that each one of you shew the same diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that ye be not sluggish, but imitators of those who through faith and patience have been inheritors of the promises.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: ForGod, having promised to Abraham, since he had no greater to swear by, swore by himself,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, Surely blessing I will bless thee, and multiplying I will multiply thee;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and thus, having had long patience, he got the promise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For men indeed swear by a greater, and with them the oath is a term to all dispute, as making matters sure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: WhereinGod, willing to shew more abundantly to the heirs of the promise the unchangeableness of his purpose, intervened by an oath,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: that by two unchangeable things, in which [it was] impossible thatGod should lie, we might have a strong encouragement, who have fled for refuge to lay hold on the hope set before us,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: which we have as anchor of the soul, both secure and firm, and entering into that within the veil,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: where Jesus is entered as forerunner for us, become for ever a high priest according to the order of Melchisedec.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this Melchisedec, King of Salem, priest of the most highGod, who met Abraham returning from smiting the kings, and blessed him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to whom Abraham gave also the tenth portion of all; first being interpreted King of righteousness, and then also King of Salem, which is King of peace;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: without father, without mother, without genealogy; having neither beginning of days nor end of life, but assimilated to the Son ofGod, abides a priest continually.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now consider how great this [personage] was, to whom [even] the patriarch Abraham gave a tenth out of the spoils.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they indeed from among the sons of Levi, who receive the priesthood, have commandment to take tithes from the people according to the law, that is from their brethren, though these are come out of the loins of Abraham:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he who has no genealogy from them has tithed Abraham, and blessed him who had the promises.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But beyond all gainsaying, the inferior is blessed by the better.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And here dying men receive tithes; but there [one] of whom the witness is that he lives;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, so to speak, through Abraham, Levi also, who received tithes, has been made to pay tithes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he was yet in the loins of his father when Melchisedec met him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If indeed then perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, for the people had their law given to them in connexion with it, what need [was there] still that a different priest should arise according to the order of Melchisedec, and not be named after the order of Aaron?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For, the priesthood being changed, there takes place of necessity a change of law also.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For he, of whom these things are said, belongs to a different tribe, of which no one has [ever] been attached to the service of the altar.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is clear that our Lord has sprung out of Juda, as to which tribe Moses spake nothing as to priests.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And it is yet more abundantly evident, since a different priest arises according to the similitude of Melchisedec,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who has been constituted not according to law of fleshly commandment, but according to power of indissoluble life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For it is borne witness, Thou art a priest for ever according to the order of Melchisedec.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For there is a setting aside of the commandment going before for its weakness and unprofitableness,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for the law perfected nothing,) and the introduction of a better hope by which we draw nigh toGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And by how much [it was] not without the swearing of an oath;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for they are become priests without the swearing of an oath, but he with the swearing of an oath, by him who said, as to him, The Lord has sworn, and will not repent [of it], Thou [art] priest for ever [according to the order of Melchisedec];)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: by so much Jesus became surety of a better covenant.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they have been many priests, on account of being hindered from continuing by death;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but he, because of his continuing for ever, has the priesthood unchangeable.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whence also he is able to save completely those who approach by him toGod, always living to intercede for them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For such a high priest became us, holy, harmless, undefiled, separated from sinners, and become higher than the heavens:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who has not day by day need, as the high priests, first to offer up sacrifices for his own sins, then [for] those of the people; for this he did once for all [in] having offered up himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the law constitutes men high priests, having infirmity; but the word of the swearing of the oath which [is] after the law, a Son perfected for ever.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now a summary of the things of which we are speaking [is], We have such a one high priest who has sat down on [the] right hand of the throne of the greatness in the heavens;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: minister of the holy places and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord has pitched, [and] not man.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every high priest is constituted for the offering both of gifts and sacrifices; whence it is needful that this one also should have something which he may offer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: If then indeed he were upon earth, he would not even be a priest, there being those who offer the gifts according to the law,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (who serve the representation and shadow of heavenly things, according as Moses was oracularly told [when] about to make the tabernacle; for See, saith He, that thou make all things according to the pattern which has been shewn to thee in the mountain.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But now he has got a more excellent ministry, by so much as he is mediator of a better covenant, which is established on the footing of better promises.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if that first was faultless, place had not been sought for a second.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For finding fault, he says to them, Behold, days come, saith the Lord, and I will consummate a new covenant as regards the house of Israel, and as regards the house of Juda;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not according to the covenant which I made to their fathers in [the] day of my taking their hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they did not continue in my covenant, and I did not regard them, saith [the] Lord.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because this [is] the covenant that I will covenant to the house of Israel after those days, saith the Lord: Giving my laws into their mind, I will write them also upon their hearts; and I will be to them forGod, and they shall be to me for people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And they shall not teach each his fellow-citizen, and each his brother, saying, Know the Lord; because all shall know me in themselves, from [the] little one [among them] unto [the] great among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because I will be merciful to their unrighteousnesses, and their sins and their lawlessnesses I will never remember any more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In that he says New, he has made the first old; but that which grows old and aged [is] near disappearing.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The first therefore also indeed had ordinances of service, and the sanctuary, a worldly one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a tabernacle was set up; the first, in which [were] both the candlestick and the table and the exposition of the loaves, which is called Holy;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but after the second veil a tabernacle which is called Holy of holies,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: having a golden censer, and the ark of the covenant, covered round in every part with gold, in which [were] the golden pot that had the manna, and the rod of Aaron that had sprouted, and the tables of the covenant;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and above over it the cherubim of glory shadowing the mercy-seat; concerning which it is not now [the time] to speak in detail.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now these things being thus ordered, into the first tabernacle the priests enter at all times, accomplishing the services;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but into the second, the high priest only, once a year, not without blood, which he offers for himself and for the errors of the people:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the Holy Spirit shewing this, that the way of the [holy of] holies has not yet been made manifest while as yet the first tabernacle has [its] standing;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the which [is] an image for the present time, according to which both gifts and sacrifices, unable to perfect as to conscience him that worshipped, are offered,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [consisting] only of meats and drinks and divers washings, ordinances of flesh, imposed until [the] time of setting things right.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But Christ being come high priest of the good things to come, by the better and more perfect tabernacle not made with hand, (that is, not of this creation,)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor by blood of goats and calves, but by his own blood, has entered in once for all into the [holy of] holies, having found an eternal redemption.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For if the blood of goats and bulls, and a heifer's ashes sprinkling the defiled, sanctifies for the purity of the flesh,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: how much rather shall the blood of the Christ, who by the eternal Spirit offered himself spotless toGod, purify your conscience from dead works to worship [the] livingGod?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And for this reason he is mediator of a new covenant, so that, death having taken place for redemption of the transgressions under the first covenant, the called might receive the promise of the eternal inheritance.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (For where [there is] a testament, the death of the testator must needs come in.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For a testament [is] of force when men are dead, since it is in no way of force while the testator is alive.)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Whence neither the first was inaugurated without blood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For every commandment having been spoken according to [the] law by Moses to all the people; having taken the blood of calves and goats, with water and scarlet wool and hyssop, he sprinkled both the book itself and all the people,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: saying, This [is] the blood of the covenant whichGod has enjoined to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the tabernacle too and all the vessels of service he sprinkled in like manner with blood;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and almost all things are purified with blood according to the law, and without blood-shedding there is no remission.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [It was] necessary then that the figurative representations of the things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with sacrifices better than these.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Christ is not entered into holy places made with hand, figures of the true, but into heaven itself, now to appear before the face ofGod for us:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: nor in order that he should offer himself often, as the high priest enters into the holy places every year with blood not his own;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: since he had [then] been obliged often to suffer from the foundation of the world. But now once in the consummation of the ages he has been manifested for [the] putting away of sin by his sacrifice.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And forasmuch as it is the portion of men once to die, and after this judgment;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: thus the Christ also, having been once offered to bear the sins of many, shall appear to those that look for him the second time without sin for salvation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the law, having a shadow of the coming good things, not the image itself of the things, can never, by the same sacrifices which they offer continually yearly, perfect those who approach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Since, would they not indeed have ceased being offered, on account of the worshippers once purged having no longer any conscience of sins?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But in these [there is] a calling to mind of sins yearly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For blood of bulls and goats [is] incapable of taking away sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore coming into the world he says, Sacrifice and offering thou willedst not; but thou hast prepared me a body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou tookest no pleasure in burnt-offerings and sacrifices for sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Then I said, Lo, I come (in [the] roll of the book it is written of me) to do, OGod, thy will.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Above, saying Sacrifices and offerings and burnt-offerings and sacrifices for sin thou willedst not, neither tookest pleasure in (which are offered according to the law);

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: then he said, Lo, I come to do thy will. He takes away the first that he may establish the second;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: by which will we have been sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once for all.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And every priest stands daily ministering, and offering often the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But he, having offered one sacrifice for sins, sat down in perpetuity at [the] right hand ofGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: waiting from henceforth until his enemies be set [for the] footstool of his feet.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For by one offering he has perfected in perpetuity the sanctified.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the Holy Spirit also bears us witness [of it]; for after what was said:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: This [is] the covenant which I will establish towards them after those days, saith [the] Lord: Giving my laws into their hearts, I will write them also in their understandings;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and their sins and their lawlessnesses I will never remember any more.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But where there [is] remission of these, [there is] no longer a sacrifice for sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Having therefore, brethren, boldness for entering into the [holy of] holies by the blood of Jesus,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the new and living way which he has dedicated for us through the veil, that is, his flesh,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and [having] a great priest over the house ofGod,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: let us approach with a true heart, in full assurance of faith, sprinkled as to our hearts from a wicked conscience, and washed as to our body with pure water.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us hold fast the confession of the hope unwavering, (for he [is] faithful who has promised;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and let us consider one another for provoking to love and good works;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the custom [is] with some; but encouraging [one another], and by so much the more as ye see the day drawing near.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For where we sin wilfully after receiving the knowledge of the truth, there no longer remains any sacrifice for sins,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but a certain fearful expectation of judgment, and heat of fire about to devour the adversaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Any one that has disregarded Moses' law dies without mercy on [the testimony of] two or three witnesses:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: of how much worse punishment, think ye, shall he be judged worthy who has trodden under foot the Son ofGod, and esteemed the blood of the covenant, whereby he has been sanctified, common, and has insulted the Spirit of grace?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For we know him that said, To me [belongs] vengeance; I will recompense, saith the Lord: and again, The Lord shall judge his people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [It is] a fearful thing falling into [the] hands of [the] livingGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But call to mind the earlier days in which, having been enlightened, ye endured much conflict of sufferings;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: on the one hand, when ye were made a spectacle both in reproaches and afflictions; and on the other, when ye became partakers with those who were passing through them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye both sympathised with prisoners and accepted with joy the plunder of your goods, knowing that ye have for yourselves a better substance, and an abiding one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which has great recompense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye have need of endurance in order that, having done the will ofGod, ye may receive the promise.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For yet a very little while he that comes will come, and will not delay.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But the just shall live by faith; and, if he draw back, my soul does not take pleasure in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But we are not drawers back to perdition, but of faith to saving [the] soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Now faith is [the] substantiating of things hoped for, [the] conviction of things not seen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For in [the power of] this the elders have obtained testimony.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith we apprehend that the worlds were framed by [the] word ofGod, so that that which is seen should not take its origin from things which appear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Abel offered toGod a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained testimony of being righteous,God bearing testimony to his gifts, and by it, having died, he yet speaks.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, becauseGod had translated him; for before [his] translation he has the testimony that he had pleasedGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But without faith [it is] impossible to please [him]. For he that draws near toGod must believe that he is, and [that] he is a rewarder of them who seek him out.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith, Noah, oracularly warned concerning things not yet seen, moved with fear, prepared an ark for the saving of his house; by which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which [is] according to faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Abraham, being called, obeyed to go out into the place which he was to receive for an inheritance, and went out, not knowing where he was going.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith he sojourned as a stranger in the land of promise as a foreign country, having dwelt in tents with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with [him] of the same promise;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he waited for the city which has foundations, of whichGod is [the] artificer and constructor.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith also Sarah herself received strength for [the] conception of seed, and [that] beyond a seasonable age; since she counted him faithful who promised.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore also there have been born of one, and that of one become dead, even as the stars of heaven in multitude, and as the countless sand which [is] by the sea shore.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All these died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them from afar off and embraced [them], and confessed that they were strangers and sojourners on the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they who say such things shew clearly that they seek [their] country.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And if they had called to mind that from whence they went out, they had had opportunity to have returned;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but now they seek a better, that is, a heavenly; whereforeGod is not ashamed of them, to be called theirGod; for he has prepared for them a city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Abraham, [when] tried, offered up Isaac, and he who had received to himself the promises offered up his only begotten [son],

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: as to whom it had been said, In Isaac shall thy seed be called:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: counting thatGod [was] able to raise [him] even from among [the] dead, whence also he received him in a figure.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Jacob [when] dying blessed each of the sons of Joseph, and worshipped on the top of his staff.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Joseph [when] dying called to mind the going forth of the sons of Israel, and gave commandment concerning his bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Moses, being born, was hid three months by his parents, because they saw the child beautiful; and they did not fear the injunction of the king.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Moses, when he had become great, refused to be called son of Pharaoh's daughter;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: choosing rather to suffer affliction along with the people ofGod than to have [the] temporary pleasure of sin;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: esteeming the reproach of the Christ greater riches than the treasures of Egypt, for he had respect to the recompense.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith he left Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king; for he persevered, as seeing him who is invisible.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith he celebrated the passover and the sprinkling of the blood, that the destroyer of the firstborn might not touch them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith they passed through the Red sea as through dry land; of which the Egyptians having made trial were swallowed up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith the walls of Jericho fell, having been encircled for seven days.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By faith Rahab the harlot did not perish along with the unbelieving, having received the spies in peace.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And what more do I say? For the time would fail me telling of Gideon, and Barak, and Samson, and Jephthah, and David and Samuel, and of the prophets:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: who by faith overcame kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped lions' mouths,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: quenched [the] power of fire, escaped [the] edge of the sword, became strong out of weakness, became mighty in war, made [the] armies of strangers give way.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Women received their dead again by resurrection; and others were tortured, not having accepted deliverance, that they might get a better resurrection;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and others underwent trial of mockings and scourgings, yea, and of bonds and imprisonment.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They were stoned, were sawn asunder, were tempted, died by the death of the sword; they went about in sheepskins, in goatskins, destitute, afflicted, evil treated,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (of whom the world was not worthy,) wandering in deserts and mountains, and [in] dens and caverns of the earth.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And these all, having obtained witness through faith, did not receive the promise,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: God having foreseen some better thing for us, that they should not be made perfect without us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us also therefore, having so great a cloud of witnesses surrounding us, laying aside every weight, and sin which so easily entangles us, run with endurance the race that lies before us,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: looking stedfastly on Jesus the leader and completer of faith: who, in view of the joy lying before him, endured [the] cross, having despised [the] shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne ofGod.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For consider well him who endured so great contradiction from sinners against himself, that ye be not weary, fainting in your minds.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye have not yet resisted unto blood, wrestling against sin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And ye have quite forgotten the exhortation which speaks to you as to sons: My son, despise not [the] chastening of [the] Lord, nor faint [when] reproved by him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for whom [the] Lord loves he chastens, and scourges every son whom he receives.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Ye endure for chastening,God conducts himself towards you as towards sons; for who is the son that the father chastens not?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But if ye are without chastening, of which all have been made partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sons.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Moreover we have had the fathers of our flesh as chasteners, and we reverenced [them]; shall we not much rather be in subjection to the Father of spirits, and live?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For they indeed chastened for a few days, as seemed good to them; but he for profit, in order to the partaking of his holiness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But no chastening at the time seems to be [matter] of joy, but of grief; but afterwards yields [the] peaceful fruit of righteousness to those exercised by it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore lift up the hands that hang down, and the failing knees;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and make straight paths for your feet, that that which is lame be not turned aside; but that rather it may be healed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pursue peace with all, and holiness, without which no one shall see the Lord:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: watching lest [there be] any one who lacks the grace ofGod; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble [you], and many be defiled by it;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: lest [there be] any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one meal sold his birthright;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for ye know that also afterwards, desiring to inherit the blessing, he was rejected, (for he found no place for repentance) although he sought it earnestly with tears.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For ye have not come to [the mount] that might be touched and was all on fire, and to obscurity, and darkness, and tempest,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and trumpet's sound, and voice of words; which they that heard, excusing themselves, declined [the] word being addressed to them any more:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: (for they were not able to bear what was enjoined: And if a beast should touch the mountain, it shall be stoned;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and, so fearful was the sight, Moses said, I am exceedingly afraid and full of trembling;)

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but ye have come to mount Zion; and to [the] city of [the] livingGod, heavenly Jerusalem; and to myriads of angels,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the universal gathering; and to [the] assembly of the firstborn [who are] registered in heaven; and toGod, judge of all; and to [the] spirits of just [men] made perfect;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: and to Jesus, mediator of a new covenant; and to [the] blood of sprinkling, speaking better than Abel.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See that ye refuse not him that speaks. For if those did not escape who had refused him who uttered the oracles on earth, much more we who turn away from him [who does so] from heaven:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: whose voice then shook the earth; but now he has promised, saying, Yet once will I shake not only the earth, but also the heaven.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But this Yet once, signifies the removing of what is shaken, as being made, that what is not shaken may remain.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore let us, receiving a kingdom not to be shaken, have grace, by which let us serveGod acceptably with reverence and fear.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For also ourGod [is] a consuming fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let brotherly love abide.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not forgetful of hospitality; for by it some have unawares entertained angels.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember prisoners, as bound with [them]; those that are evil-treated, as being yourselves also in [the] body.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Let] marriage [be held] every way in honour, and the bed [be] undefiled; for fornicators and adulterers willGod judge.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [Let your] conversation [be] without love of money, satisfied with [your] present circumstances; for he has said, I will not leave thee, neither will I forsake thee.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: So that, taking courage, we may say, The Lord [is] my helper, and I will not be afraid: what will man do unto me?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember your leaders who have spoken to you the word ofGod; and considering the issue of their conversation, imitate their faith.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jesus Christ [is] the same yesterday, and to-day, and to the ages [to come].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Be not carried away with various and strange doctrines; for [it is] good that the heart be confirmed with grace, not meats; those who have walked in which have not been profited by [them].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have an altar of which they have no right to eat who serve the tabernacle;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for of those beasts whose blood is carried [as sacrifices for sin] into the [holy of] holies by the high priest, of these the bodies are burned outside the camp.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore also Jesus, that he might sanctify the people by his own blood, suffered without the gate:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: therefore let us go forth to him without the camp, bearing his reproach:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for we have not here an abiding city, but we seek the coming one.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: By him therefore let us offer [the] sacrifice of praise continually toGod, that is, [the] fruit of [the] lips confessing his name.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But of doing good and communicating [of your substance] be not forgetful, for with such sacrificesGod is well pleased.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Obey your leaders, and be submissive; for they watch over your souls as those that shall give account; that they may do this with joy, and not groaning, for this [would be] unprofitable for you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Pray for us: for we persuade ourselves that we have a good conscience, in all things desirous to walk rightly.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I much more beseech [you] to do this, that I may the more quickly be restored to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But theGod of peace, who brought again from among [the] dead our Lord Jesus, the great shepherd of the sheep, in [the power of the] blood of [the] eternal covenant,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: perfect you in every good work to the doing of his will, doing in you what is pleasing before him through Jesus Christ; to whom [be] glory for the ages of ages. Amen.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: But I beseech you, brethren, bear the word of exhortation, for it is but in few words that I have written to you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Know that our brother Timotheus is set at liberty; with whom, if he should come soon, I will see you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Salute all your leaders, and all the saints. They from Italy salute you.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Grace [be] with you all. Amen.
